steps.summary: Found 8 existing comprehension artifacts. Relevant: orchestration.md (architecture, checkpoint chain), workflow-server.md (behavioral analysis, loader patterns).
Reviewed orchestration.md (project structure, 6 layers) and workflow-server.md (data flow, design patterns). Both provide strong baseline context.
Mapped 3-layer rule architecture: global (85 rules, rules-loader), workflow (118 rules, workflow-loader), skill (formalized, skill-loader). Identified key modules and their line counts.
Documented RulesSchema (passthrough sections), skill resolution chain (3-tier lookup), and workflow vs skill rule format differences (string array vs named object).
Inferred 3 design rationale hypotheses: monolithic rules were appropriate when small, workflow rules enabled self-containment but caused duplication, skills were designed later with proper structure.

Mapped 9 domain concepts to technical constructs: global rules, workflow rules, universal skills, workflow-specific skills, skill rules, skill protocol, bootstrap payload, on-demand loading, meta exclusion.

Created new comprehension artifact with architecture overview, key abstractions, complete rule duplication map across all 10 workflows, section migration map for meta/rules.toon, and domain concept mapping.

Read all 10 workflow.toon files to build complete rule duplication map. Identified 5 duplication clusters, mapped 16 meta/rules.toon sections to stays/migrates destinations. Verified all 5 open questions through code analysis.
All 5 open questions resolved through code analysis. has_open_questions=false, needs_comprehension=false. Comprehension-sufficient checkpoint skipped per SUFFICIENCY RULE.
